channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Okpekpe International 10km Road Race Appoints New Medical Director and Announces Sponsors
item.description: The organisers of the Okpekpe International 10km Road Race don don appoint Dr. Adebukola Bojuwoye as the head of their medical team for the 11th edition wey go hold on May 30 for Okpekpe, Edo State. D...

item.title: UK unemployment rises slightly as wage growth decelerates
item.description: The latest labour market data indicates that the UK unemployment rate has increased to 5%, signalling a modest rise in joblessness. At the same time, wage growth has slowed, suggesting that the pace o...

item.title: Life and Fashion of Women on Beaches in the 1950s
item.description: Beach culture in the mid-20th century marked a significant shift for women, reflecting both social liberation and evolving fashion trends. During the 1950s, swimwear transitioned from conservative, st...

item.title: Alex Marquez and Johann Zarco Released from Hospital Following Catalan GP Accidents
item.description: Both Alex Marquez and Johann Zarco were discharged from hospital after sustaining injuries in separate incidents during the Catalan Grand Prix, which led to the race being red-flagged twice. Marquez, ...

item.title: EAC and IGAD strengthen digital integration to expand trade in Eastern Africa
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) together with the Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD) are working hard to improve digital integration across Eastern Africa. This effort is meant to make...

item.title: WHO Declares Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak Spreading from DRC to Uganda as Public Health Emergency of International Concern
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has officially declared the ongoing Bundibugyo strain of Ebola virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and neighbouring Uganda as a Pub...

item.title: Experts Warn of Geopolitical Risks Amid Trump’s Recent International Actions
item.description: The article analyzes recent international actions by former President Donald Trump and their implications for global security. During a visit to Beijing, Trump expressed admiration for Chinese leader ...

item.title: Newfoundland’s Unsolved 1990s Murder Case Still Lacks Answers Despite DNA Advances
item.description: Despite advancements in forensic technology, including a significant DNA breakthrough years ago, Newfoundland and Labrador’s most infamous unsolved murder remains a mystery. The case, which captured n...

item.title: Swinney outlines plan to cap supermarket food prices amid cost of living concerns
item.description: SNP leader John Swinney has defended his party's proposal to legally cap the prices of certain essential food items in supermarkets, ahead of his expected re-election as Scotland's first minister. Spe...

item.title: Rappler Establishes Guidelines for Safe and Transparent Use of AI in Journalism
item.description: Rappler has formally outlined its policy on the use of generative artificial intelligence (AI) to ensure that technology supports rather than replaces human editorial judgment. The newsroom emphasizes...

item.title: U.S. Implements Airport Screening After Ebola Case in American Worker
item.description: A U.S. citizen working in Congo has tested positive for Ebola, prompting heightened concern among health experts. The individual is being transported to Germany for specialized treatment, alongside si...

item.title: Jeridah Andayi Encourages Women with Obstetric Fistula to Seek Free Medical Treatment
item.description: Former Citizen Radio presenter Jeridah Andayi has spoken out about obstetric fistula, urging women who are suffering to seek medical attention instead of staying quiet. Obstetric fistula is a childbir...

item.title: Philippine Congress Proposes Bills to Regulate Malicious Deepfakes
item.description: Philippine lawmakers are moving to regulate deepfake technology due to its increasing misuse in disinformation, fraud, harassment, and sexual exploitation. High-profile figures, including President Bo...

item.title: National Television Awards 2026 longlist nominations revealed, including new Reality Docuseries category
item.description: The National Television Awards (NTAs) have released their longlist nominations for the 31st annual ceremony, scheduled to take place on 8 September. Prominent names in the Best TV Presenter category i...

item.title: Man charged with murder of wife and two children in Campbelltown
item.description: NSW Police have charged a 47-year-old man with three counts of murder after the bodies of his wife and two sons, aged four and 12, were found at a home in Campbelltown, south-west Sydney. Emergency se...

item.title: Social Media Campaign Highlights Reactions to Budget Capital Gains Tax Proposal
item.description: Following the recent federal budget announcement, a social media campaign has gained widespread attention by portraying an AI-generated version of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese as co-founder of vari...

item.title: Starbucks Korea CEO Fired After Controversial ‘Tank Day’ Promotion
item.description: Starbucks Korea don dismiss their CEO, Son Jung-hyun, after wahala burst over dem new coffee cup campaign wey dem call 'Tank Day'. The tins pain people because the campaign launch land for May 18, wey...

item.title: Transforming Discarded Photographs into New Artistic Compositions
item.description: Cynthia Katz explores the creative potential hidden within discarded artworks in her series 'Almost Gone.' She salvages fragments of images originally considered failures, which might have been torn u...

item.title: 2026 World Cup Group B Preview: Can Canada Make it Through?
item.description: Group B of the 2026 FIFA World Cup features hosts Canada alongside Bosnia and Herzegovina, Qatar, and Switzerland. While Canada is not traditionally seen as a football powerhouse, soccer is popular am...

item.title: Ways Form 6 leavers can gain skills and experience before university
item.description: Dar es Salaam. After the 2026 Advanced Certificate of Secondary Education Examination (ACSEE), more than 133,000 students are back home waiting for results. Experts say this waiting period is a golden...
